message:
fix(choreography): first_active_participant traverses inert Seq/Par prefixes

Seq and Par variants previously returned first/left.first_active_participant()
without falling through to second/right when the prefix was inert (e.g. End).
This caused knowledge-of-choice validation to miss violations where a chooser
delegates the first communication in a branch to a non-chooser via an inert
Seq or Par prefix.

- Seq::first_active_participant now chains .or_else(|| second.first_active_participant())
- Par::first_active_participant now chains .or_else(|| right.first_active_participant())
- is_deadlock_free() strengthened: now returns true only when validate() is empty,
  not just when no KnowledgeOfChoice errors exist (other validation errors also
  indicate potential deadlocks)
- Existing negative tests gain assert!(!protocol.is_deadlock_free()) to cover
  the strengthened predicate
- Two new tests for Seq/Par inert-prefix knowledge-of-choice violations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
